Keza, yine vatandaşa, bu kanun teklifinde getirilen yeni yüklerle yapılması gereken düzenlemelerin yapılmayarak daha çok eziyetçi bir anlayışla vergiyi yaymak vergiyle ilgili kaçakları önler. Sürekli kayıt dışından söz ediliyor, yirmi üç yıldır şu kayıt dışıyla nasıl baş edileceği konusunda teknik bir anlayış ve çalışma gerçekleştirilmedi. Birkaç tane böyle, yama niteliğinde çalışma olsa da bütünüyle ilgili baktığımız zaman sorunlar var. Türkiye, bir ithalatçı yaklaşım ve anlayıştan tabii ki kurtulamadı, her alanda dışa bağımlılığını artırdığı ve kendi değerlerini sahiplenmediği için de ülkenin içinde bulunduğu sorunlar da artarak devam ediyor.
Bu nedenle, bu vergi kanununun geri çekilmesi, yeniden düzenlenmesine ihtiyaç olduğunu düşünüyorum. Biraz evvelki konuşmada gördüğümüz kadarıyla Cumhur İttifakı'nın diğer ortağı da buradaki yapılan düzenlemelere bazı maddeleri için karşı çıktı. Bunun Komisyona çekilmesinde fayda var. Ayrıca, bu konuda...
(Mikrofon otomatik cihaz tarafından kapatıldı)
ÖMER FETHİ GÜRER (Niğde) - Bir son bağlantı yapalım.
MUSTAFA KALAYCI (Konya) - Karşı çıkma yok, izahat getirildi.
ÖMER FETHİ GÜRER (Niğde) - İşte, o karşı çıkma yok ama izahat da karşı çıkmanın başka bir tanımı.
Bu konuda son söz, ilgili demokratik kitle örgütlerinin görüşleri de kapsamlı biçimde alınmalı. Bu vergi hepimizi ilgilendiriyor. Gelir artırıcı değil geliri daraltıcı bir süreç, Türkiye'deki sorunları artırır, yoksulluğu derinleştirir.
OTURUM BAŞKANI ORHAN ERDEM - Sayın Gürer, tamamlayalım.
ÖMER FETHİ GÜRER (Niğde) - Bugün açlık ve yoksulluk sınırı altında milyonlarca insan inim inim inlerken onların cebindeki son kuruşu alma anlayışıyla iktidarın vatandaşın cebine elini sokmaktan vazgeçtiği bir sürecin olmasını temenni ediyor, teşekkür ediyorum.
records.englishTranslation:
ÖMER FETHI GÜRER (Niğde) - Thank you, Mr. President.
A new omnibus bill proposal. Opposition bill proposals are waiting in the commissions, they are not discussed, we only discuss the bill proposals brought by AK PARTY deputies. In the presidential system, the bureaucrat prepares the proposal, the member of parliament signs it and brings it to the commission, the appointed minister will be the implementer, but even he does not come to the commission. Even though there are valuable bureaucrats here, representation there is reduced to representation by assistants or unit managers. Even the relevant senior managers do not come to the commission. Let's assume that one or two of them have an important meeting or duty. Almost no senior officials are involved in the work here.
When the tax-related law comes to the Parliamentary Commission, "Is there a regulation regarding tax justice?" We look, but there is none. Tax has become a tyranny for those with low and fixed incomes. The 36-article proposal brings additional burden and burden to the citizens. During the AKP period, a bill comes to the commission, the law is accepted by the majority in the Parliament, and in the meantime, the Constitutional Court either cancels it or the government gives up on what it had brought. The question "Is it against the Constitution or not?" about the legislative proposals annulled by the Constitutional Court. It seems that even bureaucrats did not examine it.
Insurance regulations seem to create new problems. The expectations of large segments are not included in the bill. Our President tweeted about three years ago, "Good news for tradesmen, we reduced the premium from 9000 days to 7200 days." he said. Nearly three years have passed, and there is still no regulation regarding this. After women start working, let's say a week later, they go on leave due to maternity period, they can get into debt and count it towards retirement, but the woman who starts working cannot get into debt if she has maternity period during the pre-employment period. Regarding this, women have been struggling and expecting for years, but these are not being met. There is a forgotten section of the EYT in the legal regulation enacted for the section we call "justice in retirement" or "those stuck in history", those who lost seventeen years in one day. Here again, the expectation of a regulation for a gradual transition was not included in this bill. There are those who define themselves as "victims of internships and apprenticeships" and who want retirement-related old-age insurance to start on the day they start working. "Let's pay our retroactive premium." They say, they want this process to start regarding us after we pay our premiums, but there doesn't seem to be any work on it.
Dear friends, taxes are meaningful when they are collected fairly. It is painful and thought-provoking, the Coach of the National Team is among the tax record holders, so Adana is where he is. Adana was an important center that could be considered the capital of industry, so the industrialists are in such a difficult situation that now we are talking about the taxes paid by the artillerymen or coaches. Also, every year, different studies are conducted around the world about Türkiye's rich people. So we don't see the names of those rich people on the tax lists.
They do not invest their earnings outside Türkiye within Türkiye. But besides this, the manufacturers of Turkish products are changing hands.

Previously, there were billions called "Turquality", supported and paid for the representation of the Turkish brand abroad, but today the organizations to which we paid those billions have turned into foreign organizations. Every poor person here and there, a strange group of people, also has rights. Unfortunately, there are problems that arise because a correct method has not been developed in the regulations made.
Likewise, by not making the necessary arrangements for the new burdens imposed on the citizens in this bill, and spreading the tax in a more oppressive manner will prevent tax-related evasion. There is constant talk of the unregistered. For twenty-three years, no technical understanding or study has been carried out on how to deal with this unregistered situation. Even though there are a few such patchwork studies, there are problems when we look at the whole. Of course, Türkiye could not get rid of an importer approach and understanding, and since it increased its foreign dependency in every field and did not embrace its own values, the problems faced by the country continue to increase.
Therefore, I think there is a need to withdraw and reorganize this tax law. As far as we saw in the previous speech, the other partner of the People's Alliance also opposed the regulations made here for some of their articles. It would be beneficial to bring this to the Commission. Also, for that matter.
(The microphone was automatically turned off by the device)
ÖMER FETHI GÜRER (Niğde) - Let's make one last connection.
MUSTAFA KALAYCI (Konya) - There is no objection, an explanation has been given.
ÖMER FETHI GÜRER (Niğde) - Well, there is no opposition, but the explanation is another definition of opposition.
The final say on this issue should be the opinions of the relevant democratic mass organizations in a comprehensive manner. This tax concerns us all. A process that reduces income rather than increasing income will increase the problems in Türkiye and deepen poverty.
CHAIRMAN OF THE SESSION ORHAN ERDEM - Mr. Gürer, let's finish.
ÖMER FETHİ GÜRER (Niğde) - Today, while millions of people are groaning under the hunger and poverty line, I hope for a process in which the government stops putting its hand into the pockets of the citizens with the understanding of taking the last penny from their pockets, and I thank you.

fields.Subject: 2026 Yılı Merkezi Yönetim Bütçe Kanunu Teklifi (1/280) ve 2024 Yılı Merkezi Yönetim Kesin Hesap Kanunu Teklifi (1/279) ile Sayıştay tezkereleri münasebetiyle

records.originalTurkish:
ÖMER FETHİ GÜRER (Niğde) - Teşekkürler Başkanım.
2026 bütçesi işçi, çiftçi, esnaf, memur, emekli, işsiz, kadın, genç, engelli, hatta sanayici için bir umut bütçesi değil; staj ve çırak mağdurları, emeklilikte adalet arayan ve kademeli emeklilik bekleyenler, taşeronda kalıp kadro alamayanlar bu bütçede yer bulmuyor. 2026'da, bütçede, rahat yüzü görmeyenler için yine bir umut yok.
Tarım açısından bakarsak durum daha da vahim. Tarım Kanunu'nda millî gelirin yüzde 1'inin çiftçiye destek verileceği yazıyor. 2026 yılı millî gelirinin yüzde 1'i 722 milyar lira yapıyor, çiftçiye bütçeden ayrılan ise yalnızca 168 milyar lira. Esas düşündürücü olan, Tarım ve Orman Bakanlığının 2026 yılı bütçesi 540 milyar 840 milyon lira yani kanuna göre destek olarak vermek gereken tutardan daha az Bakanlığın bütçesi var. Destekler hasat öncesi ya da hasat sürecinde değil neredeyse bir yıl sonra veriliyor. Yılbaşında çiftçilerin bankalara borcu 899 milyar 745 milyon iken takipteki kredi borcu 4 milyar 480 milyon 112 bin liraydı, on ayda
1 trilyon 100 milyar lira borç çıkmış bulunuyor. 10 milyar 303 milyon 927 bin takipteki kredi borcu durumuna erdi. 1 trilyon 100 milyar lira da bankalara kredi borçları var. Traktöre, tarlaya haciz geliyor; yetmedi hayvanlara hatta depodaki buğdaya haciz gelen bir süreç yaşanıyor. Tüm kesimlerin bu yıl 8 milyon 654 bin icra dosyası gelmiş ve toplamda 24 milyon 781 bin 747 derdest bulunan dosya var. Geçmiş dönemde silinmelere rağmen icra dosyaları azalmıyor. Sürekli tarımda ihracatçı olduğumuzu söylüyorsunuz; fındık, kayısı, üzüm, incir, limon gibi ürünlerde ihracatçıyız doğru ama hububatta ve bakliyatta ise arz açığımız var ve ithalatçıyız. İki yılda nohutta üretim kaybımız 247 bin tonu buldu. Ana vatan ülkemiz olan mercimek de iki yıldaki kaybımız 194 bin ton, ürettiğimizin neredeyse 1 kat fazlası da ithalat yapıyoruz. Kuru fasulyede 2002 yılına kadar ürettiğimizi bu yıl da üretiyoruz, nüfusumuz 30 milyon artmış, 2002 üretiminde kalmışız. 2025 yılı Ekim ayı TÜİK verilerine göre meyvedeki kaybımız bir yılda 8 milyon tonu aştı. Kayıt dışıyla 10 milyona yakın ürün kaybı var. Elmada yüzde 48,3, kirazda yüzde 70,6, zeytinde yüzde 34,7, limonda yüzde 34,8 kayıplar yaşandı. Tahılda ve bitkisel üretimde iki yılda yaşanan kayıp da 8 milyon ton ama Bakanlığın sitesine girdiğin zaman veriler var, neredeyse her konuda yeterlilik gösterdiği dönemdeki üretime göre önemli ölçüde ürün kaybı yaşandı. 2023 yılında 22 milyon ton buğday üreten olan ülkemizde 17 milyon 900 bin tona üretim geriledi. Biri zirai donla açıklanır, biri kuraklıkla. Peki, bu buğdaydaki kayıpta çiftçiye sağlanan bir destek var mı? Yok, sıfır destek. Üretim neredeyse 4 milyon ton kaybedilmiş durumda. Hayvancılıkta da durum vahim. 2010 yılında başlayan hayvan ithalatında bugüne kadar 10 milyon baş hayvan ithal edildi, ne hayvancılıkta sorun bitti ne ette sorun bitti. Cumhurbaşkanlığı program kitabında büyükbaş hayvan varlığı 16 milyon 800 bin baş yazıyor. TÜİK'deyse 17 milyon 188 bin baş yazıyor. Bu yıl şap yaşandı, sorduk "Şaptan kaç hayvan öldü? Kaçı şartlı kesime gitti? Bu anlamda üreticinin kaybı ne? Ne kadar destek verildi." diye, açıklanmadı. Şu şaptan Türkiye'nin 81 ilinde yasaklama geldiğine göre kaç hayvan öldü? 2023 yılında 818 bin, 2024 yılında 559 bin hayvan ithal ettik. Programda bugün baktığım, okuduğuma göre 895 bin de bu yıl sonuna kadar hayvan ithalatı görülüyor. Yerli ırkımız 1 milyonun altına gerilemiş, neredeyse kendi bölgelerimize ait ırkın sonu geliyor.
Merkez Bankası Başkanı -geldiğinde- mayıs ayında Meclisteki sunumunda "Burada zirai donun önemli etkisi olmayacak." dedi, Tarım Orman Bakanı "Arz açığı bulunmayacak." dedi, Sayın Cumhurbaşkanına da bilgiler böyle arz edilmiş olmalı, o da benzer açıklamalar yaptı, sonra Merkez Bankası Başkanı bu ayın içinde geldi, zirai dondan, kuraklıktan bahsetti. Şimdi, beş ay önce bu öngörüde bulunduklarında, o zaman mayısta beni dinleselerdi Bakanlığın o kadar elemanına ya da Merkez Bankasına rağmen ben mayısta bunları anlatmıştım ve paylaşmıştım kamuoyuyla. Onun için de öngörülerde bir sorun var, verilerde bir karmaşa var. Üretici, çiftçi çalışanlar, emekliler çok zor şartlarda yaşam mücadelesi verirken fahiş fiyat mücadelesinde de sonuç alınamıyor. Türkiye israfta da yüzde 35...
(Mikrofon otomatik cihaz tarafından kapatıldı)
ÖMER FETHİ GÜRER (Niğde) - Başkanım, daha 1'inci sayfa bitti.
records.englishTranslation:
ÖMER FETHI GÜRER (Niğde) - Thank you, President.
The 2026 budget is not a budget of hope for workers, farmers, tradesmen, civil servants, retirees, unemployed, women, young people, disabled people or even industrialists; Victims of internships and apprenticeships, those who seek justice in retirement and expect gradual retirement, and those who work as subcontractors and cannot get a position do not find a place in this budget. In 2026, there is no hope for those who have no peace in the budget.
If we look at it from an agricultural perspective, the situation is even worse. It is written in the Agricultural Law that 1 percent of the national income will be supported to farmers. 1 percent of the national income of 2026 amounts to 722 billion liras, and the budget allocated to farmers is only 168 billion liras. What is really thought-provoking is that the 2026 budget of the Ministry of Agriculture and Forestry is 540 billion 840 million lira, that is, the Ministry's budget is less than the amount required to be given as support according to the law. Support is given almost a year later, not before or during the harvest. While farmers' debt to banks was 899 billion 745 million at the beginning of the year, the outstanding loan debt was 4 billion 480 million 112 thousand lira, in ten months
There is a debt of 1 trillion 100 billion liras. It reached the status of 10 billion 303 million 927 thousand non-performing loan debts. They have loan debts to banks of 1 trillion 100 billion liras. Tractors and fields are seized; As if that was not enough, there is a process in which animals and even wheat in the warehouse are seized. This year, 8 million 654 thousand enforcement files from all segments have been received and there are a total of 24 million 781 thousand 747 pending files. Despite the deletions in the past, enforcement files do not decrease. You constantly say that we are exporters in agriculture; It is true that we are exporters of products such as hazelnuts, apricots, grapes, figs and lemons, but we have a supply deficit in grains and pulses and we are importers. Our production loss in chickpeas in two years amounted to 247 thousand tons. Our loss in lentils, which is our homeland, in two years is 194 thousand tons, and we import almost 1 times more than we produce. This year, we are producing the same amount of dried beans we produced until 2002. Our population has increased by 30 million, and we have remained at the 2002 production level. According to TÜİK data for October 2025, our loss in fruit exceeded 8 million tons in one year. There is a loss of nearly 10 million products due to unregistered products. There were losses of 48.3 percent in apples, 70.6 percent in cherries, 34.7 percent in olives and 34.8 percent in lemons. The loss in grain and plant production in two years is 8 million tons, but when you go to the Ministry's website, there is data. There was a significant product loss compared to the production in the period when it was competent in almost every field. In our country, which produced 22 million tons of wheat in 2023, production decreased to 17 million 900 thousand tons. One is explained by agricultural frost, the other by drought. So, is there any support provided to farmers for this wheat loss? No, zero support. Production has been lost by almost 4 million tons. The situation in animal husbandry is also dire. In the animal imports that started in 2010, 10 million head of animals have been imported so far, and neither the problem in livestock nor the problem in meat has ended. The Presidential program book states that the cattle population is 16 million 800 thousand head. TurkStat says 17 million 188 thousand heads. This year, FMD occurred, we asked, "How many animals died due to FMD? How many went to conditional slaughter? In this sense, what is the producer's loss? How much support was given?" It was not disclosed.

Since this alum is banned in 81 provinces of Türkiye, how many animals died? We imported 818 thousand animals in 2023 and 559 thousand animals in 2024. According to what I looked at and read in the program today, animal imports are seen at 895 thousand by the end of this year. Our indigenous race has decreased below 1 million, and the race belonging to our own regions is almost coming to an end.
In his presentation to the Parliament in May, when the Central Bank Governor arrived, he said, "There will be no significant impact of agricultural frost here." said the Minister of Agriculture and Forestry, "There will be no supply deficit." He said, the information must have been presented to Mr. President like this, he made similar statements, then the President of the Central Bank came this month and talked about agricultural frost and drought. Now, when they made this prediction five months ago, if they had listened to me in May, I would have explained and shared these with the public in May, despite all the staff of the Ministry or the Central Bank. That's why there is a problem with the predictions, there is confusion in the data. While producers, farmers and retirees are struggling to survive under very difficult conditions, no results can be achieved in the fight against exorbitant prices. Türkiye is also 35 percent in waste.
(The microphone was automatically turned off by the device)
ÖMER FETHI GÜRER (Niğde) - President, the first page is over.

records.originalTurkish:
ÖMER FETHİ GÜRER (Niğde) - Niğde'de 1996 yılında temeli atılan, tabelası olan, bekçisi emekli olan havaalanı için 2026'da ön ödenek ayrıldı mı, ne zaman yapılacak?
Depremde zarar gören, iki yıldır kapalı Niğde Müzesine ne zaman yeni bina yapılacak?
Niğde Aladağlar'dan çıkıp Akdeniz'e boşa akan Ecemiş suyunun ne zaman Niğde ovalarına yönü çevrilecek?
Akkaya Barajı su dağıtımı kapalı sisteme ne zaman alınacak?
Niğde bor tarım ihtisas bölgesi ne zaman yapılacak?
Niğde'de üreticinin depoda kalan patatesinin alımına Toprak Mahsulleri Ofisi girecek mi?
Niğde il merkezi neden yüksek hızlı tren projelerinde yok?
records.englishTranslation:
ÖMER FETHI GÜRER (Niğde) - Has preliminary funding been allocated in 2026 for the airport in Niğde, whose foundations were laid in 1996, has a signboard and its guard is retired, and when will it be done?
When will the new building be built for Niğde Museum, which was damaged in the earthquake and has been closed for two years?
When will the Ecemiş water, which originates from Niğde Aladağlar and flows wastefully into the Mediterranean, be diverted to the Niğde plains?
When will Akkaya Dam water distribution be transferred to the closed system?
When will Niğde Bor agriculture specialization zone be built?
Will Soil Products Office be involved in purchasing the potatoes left in the warehouse of the producers in Niğde?
Why is Niğde city center not included in high-speed train projects?

Biri zirai donla açıklanır, biri kuraklıkla. Peki, bu buğdaydaki kayıpta çiftçiye sağlanan bir destek var mı? Yok, sıfır destek. Üretim neredeyse 4 milyon ton kaybedilmiş durumda. Hayvancılıkta da durum vahim. 2010 yılında başlayan hayvan ithalatında bugüne kadar 10 milyon baş hayvan ithal edildi, ne hayvancılıkta sorun bitti ne ette sorun bitti. Cumhurbaşkanlığı program kitabında büyükbaş hayvan varlığı 16 milyon 800 bin baş yazıyor. TÜİK'deyse 17 milyon 188 bin baş yazıyor. Bu yıl şap yaşandı, sorduk "Şaptan kaç hayvan öldü? Kaçı şartlı kesime gitti? Bu anlamda üreticinin kaybı ne? Ne kadar destek verildi." diye, açıklanmadı. Şu şaptan Türkiye'nin 81 ilinde yasaklama geldiğine göre kaç hayvan öldü? 2023 yılında 818 bin, 2024 yılında 559 bin hayvan ithal ettik. Programda bugün baktığım, okuduğuma göre 895 bin de bu yıl sonuna kadar hayvan ithalatı görülüyor. Yerli ırkımız 1 milyonun altına gerilemiş, neredeyse kendi bölgelerimize ait ırkın sonu geliyor.
Merkez Bankası Başkanı -geldiğinde- mayıs ayında Meclisteki sunumunda "Burada zirai donun önemli etkisi olmayacak." dedi, Tarım Orman Bakanı "Arz açığı bulunmayacak." dedi, Sayın Cumhurbaşkanına da bilgiler böyle arz edilmiş olmalı, o da benzer açıklamalar yaptı, sonra Merkez Bankası Başkanı bu ayın içinde geldi, zirai dondan, kuraklıktan bahsetti. Şimdi, beş ay önce bu öngörüde bulunduklarında, o zaman mayısta beni dinleselerdi Bakanlığın o kadar elemanına ya da Merkez Bankasına rağmen ben mayısta bunları anlatmıştım ve paylaşmıştım kamuoyuyla. Onun için de öngörülerde bir sorun var, verilerde bir karmaşa var. Üretici, çiftçi çalışanlar, emekliler çok zor şartlarda yaşam mücadelesi verirken fahiş fiyat mücadelesinde de sonuç alınamıyor. Türkiye israfta da yüzde 35...
(Mikrofon otomatik cihaz tarafından kapatıldı)
ÖMER FETHİ GÜRER (Niğde) - Başkanım, daha 1'inci sayfa bitti.
records.englishTranslation:
ÖMER FETHI GÜRER (Niğde) - Thank you, President.
The 2026 budget is not a budget of hope for workers, farmers, tradesmen, civil servants, retirees, unemployed, women, young people, disabled people or even industrialists; Victims of internships and apprenticeships, those who seek justice in retirement and expect gradual retirement, and those who work as subcontractors and cannot get a position do not find a place in this budget. In 2026, there is no hope for those who have no peace in the budget.
If we look at it from an agricultural perspective, the situation is even worse. It is written in the Agricultural Law that 1 percent of the national income will be supported to farmers. 1 percent of the national income of 2026 amounts to 722 billion liras, and the budget allocated to farmers is only 168 billion liras. What is really thought-provoking is that the 2026 budget of the Ministry of Agriculture and Forestry is 540 billion 840 million lira, that is, the Ministry's budget is less than the amount required to be given as support according to the law. Support is given almost a year later, not before or during the harvest. While farmers' debt to banks was 899 billion 745 million at the beginning of the year, the outstanding loan debt was 4 billion 480 million 112 thousand lira, in ten months
There is a debt of 1 trillion 100 billion lira. It reached the status of 10 billion 303 million 927 thousand non-performing loan debts. They have loan debts to banks of 1 trillion 100 billion liras. Tractors and fields are seized; As if that was not enough, there is a process in which animals and even wheat in the warehouse are seized. This year, 8 million 654 thousand enforcement files from all segments have been received and there are a total of 24 million 781 thousand 747 pending files. Despite the deletions in the past, enforcement files do not decrease. You constantly say that we are exporters in agriculture; It is true that we are exporters of products such as hazelnuts, apricots, grapes, figs and lemons, but we have a supply deficit in grains and pulses and we are importers. Our production loss in chickpeas in two years amounted to 247 thousand tons. Our loss in lentils, which is our homeland, in two years is 194 thousand tons, and we import almost 1 times more than we produce. This year, we are producing the same amount of dried beans we produced until 2002. Our population has increased by 30 million, and we have remained at the 2002 production level. According to TÜİK data for October 2025, our loss in fruit exceeded 8 million tons in one year. There is a loss of nearly 10 million products due to unregistered products. There were losses of 48.3 percent in apples, 70.6 percent in cherries, 34.7 percent in olives and 34.8 percent in lemons. The loss in grain and plant production in two years is 8 million tons, but when you go to the Ministry's website, there is data. There was a significant product loss compared to the production in the period when it was competent in almost every field. In our country, which produced 22 million tons of wheat in 2023, production decreased to 17 million 900 thousand tons. One is explained by agricultural frost, the other by drought. So, is there any support provided to farmers for this wheat loss? No, zero support. Production has been lost by almost 4 million tons. The situation in animal husbandry is also dire. In the animal imports that started in 2010, 10 million head of animals have been imported so far, and neither the problem in livestock nor the problem in meat has ended. The Presidential program book states that the cattle population is 16 million 800 thousand head. TurkStat says 17 million 188 thousand heads. This year, FMD occurred, we asked, "How many animals died due to FMD? How many went to conditional slaughter? In this sense, what is the producer's loss? How much support was given?" It was not disclosed.

Since this alum is banned in 81 provinces of Türkiye, how many animals died? We imported 818 thousand animals in 2023 and 559 thousand animals in 2024. According to what I looked at and read in the program today, animal imports are seen at 895 thousand by the end of this year. Our indigenous race has decreased below 1 million, and the race belonging to our own regions is almost coming to an end.
In his presentation to the Parliament in May, when the Central Bank Governor arrived, he said, "There will be no significant impact of agricultural frost here." said the Minister of Agriculture and Forestry, "There will be no supply deficit." He said, the information must have been presented to Mr. President like this, he made similar statements, then the President of the Central Bank came this month and talked about agricultural frost and drought. Now, when they made this prediction five months ago, if they had listened to me in May, I would have explained and shared these with the public in May, despite all the staff of the Ministry or the Central Bank. That's why there is a problem with the predictions, there is confusion in the data. While producers, farmers and retirees are struggling to survive under very difficult conditions, no results can be achieved in the fight against exorbitant prices. Türkiye is also 35 percent in waste.
(The microphone was automatically turned off by the device)
ÖMER FETHI GÜRER (Niğde) - President, the first page is over.

fields.Subject: 2026 Yılı Merkezi Yönetim Bütçe Kanunu Teklifi (1/280) ve 2024 Yılı Merkezi Yönetim Kesin Hesap Kanunu Teklifi (1/279) ile Sayıştay tezkereleri

records.originalTurkish:
ÖMER FETHİ GÜRER (Niğde) - Niğde'de 1996 yılında temeli atılan, tabelası olan, bekçisi emekli olan havaalanı için 2026'da ön ödenek ayrıldı mı, ne zaman yapılacak?
Depremde zarar gören, iki yıldır kapalı Niğde Müzesine ne zaman yeni bina yapılacak?
Niğde Aladağlar'dan çıkıp Akdeniz'e boşa akan Ecemiş suyunun ne zaman Niğde ovalarına yönü çevrilecek?
Akkaya Barajı su dağıtımı kapalı sisteme ne zaman alınacak?
Niğde bor tarım ihtisas bölgesi ne zaman yapılacak?
Niğde'de üreticinin depoda kalan patatesinin alımına Toprak Mahsulleri Ofisi girecek mi?
Niğde il merkezi neden yüksek hızlı tren projelerinde yok?
records.englishTranslation:
ÖMER FETHI GÜRER (Niğde) - Has preliminary funding been allocated in 2026 for the airport in Niğde, whose foundations were laid in 1996, has a signboard and its guard is retired, and when will it be done?
When will the new building be built for Niğde Museum, which was damaged in the earthquake and has been closed for two years?
When will the Ecemiş water, which originates from Niğde Aladağlar and flows wastefully into the Mediterranean, be diverted to the Niğde plains?
When will Akkaya Dam water distribution be transferred to the closed system?
When will Niğde Bor agriculture specialization zone be built?
Will Soil Products Office be involved in purchasing the potatoes left in the warehouse of the producers in Niğde?
Why is Niğde city center not included in high-speed train projects?

fields.Subject: 2026 Yılı Merkezi Yönetim Bütçe Kanunu Teklifi (1/280) ve 2024 Yılı Merkezi Yönetim Kesin Hesap Kanunu Teklifi (1/279) ile Sayıştay tezkereleri münasebetiyle

records.originalTurkish:
ÖMER FETHİ GÜRER (Niğde) - Sayın Bakan, toplumun farklı kesimleri enflasyon ve sıkı para politikası etkisiyle büyük sıkıntı yaşamaktadır. 2024 yılından başlayarak yüksek faizin de etkisiyle sanayicilerde de ciddi sorunlar oluşmaktadır. İmalat sanayisinde gerileme devam etmektedir, bazı tekstil fabrikaları ülke dışına taşınırken bazı fabrikalar dağılma yoluna gitmektedir. Bu süreç geçici daralma değil, kalıcı bir dönüşüm sürecine dönerse Türkiye'de önemli ölçüde işsizlik sorunu daha da derinleşecektir. Fabrikalar ham madde alırken ödedikleri KDV'nin üretim süreci ve pazarlama noktasına kadar sanayici için ek bir külfete neden olduğunu, KDV oran farklarının alış satış sürecini olumsuz etkilediğini belirtmektedirler. Bu konuda sizin de Hazine ve Maliye Bakanlığı nezdinde girişimlerde bulunmanızı talep ediyorlar. Sanayi ve Teknoloji Bakanlığınca bazı uygulamalar için de idari para cezaları kesilmektedir. Bu cezaların son iki yılda toplamı nedir, ne kadarı tahsil edilmiştir?
Ölçü ve tartı aletleri ve ambalajlarla ilgili sorunlar da tüketici şikâyetleri arasındadır. Ölçü ve tartı aletlerinin denetimi sürekli ve düzenli yapılmalıdır. Yapılanlar yeterli olmuyor ki şikâyetler bize geliyor. Bu bağlamda ambalajda gramaj oyunları da tüketiciyi yanıltmaktadır. Kap boyutu aynı korunup ürün gramajı düşürülerek dolaylı fiyat artışları gerçekleştirilmekte, üretici boyutuna bakarak aynı ürünü aldığını sanmakta ama gramaj oyunuyla da vatandaş bu yönlü de sıkıntıya sokulmaktadır.
Faaliyet raporunuzda kurum kültürünün ve aidiyetinin güçlendirilmesi alanında yetkin personel sayısının artırılmasına ihtiyaç olduğu, mevcut personelin yetkinliğinin geliştirilmesi için hizmet içi eğitimin ihtiyaç olduğu da yer almaktadır. Personelin yetkin ve etkin olmasının önemli olduğunu, bu konudaki talepleri kendiniz saptadığınız için giderilmesinin önemli olduğunu düşünüyorum.
Ayrıca Sayın Bakan, bölgeme yönelik "adı var, uygulaması yok" projeler var. Bu projelerin ne olduğunu bir kez daha size sormak istiyorum: Ulukışla ilçesi Endüstriyel Park Projesi hangi aşamadadır? 2015 yılında Niğde ili Bor ilçesinde kurulacağı açıklanan enerji ihtisas endüstri bölgesiyle ilgili son durum nedir? Niğde Bor Deri Entegre Sanayi
Yatırım Projesi sanayi bölgesi olmasına rağmen ne aşamadadır? Bor Ticaret ve Sanayi Odasının ve Bor Ziraat Odası Başkanlığının proje ortağı bulunduğu Niğde Bor Besi Organize Sanayi Bölgesi Projesi için 2026 yılı bütçesinden kaynak ayrılmış mıdır, bu bağlamda yatırım programlarında yer almakta mıdır? Son iki yılda faaliyeti sonlanan fabrika sayısı kaçtır? Konkordato ilan eden fabrika sayısı kaçtır? Bu bağlamda bir destek oluşturulmakta mıdır? Bu konkordatoyla ilgili de şöyle bir sorun anlatıyor sanayici haklı olarak: Konkordatoyu fabrika ilan ediyor ama onun aldığı, alışveriş yaptığı diğerleri de ödeme süreci gerçekleşmeyince onunla beraber sıkıntıya giriyorlar. Bu sürecin de yönetilmesinde problemler olduğu belirtiliyor. 6948 sayılı Sanayi Sicil Kanunu gereği sanayi işletmelerinin firma, şirket fabrikalarından kaçı yabancı uyruklu kişilere, şirketlere ve kuruluşlara satılmıştır? Türk markası olduğu hâlde şu anda 2025 yılında çok sayıda fabrikanın yabancılar tarafından alındığı, firmaların el değiştirdiği belirtilmektedir. Bununla ilgili sizde veri var mıdır? Bu verileri de bizimle paylaşırsanız memnun oluruz.
Ayrıca, Sayın Bakan, konuşmanızın çoğu yerinde geleceğe dönük tanımlamalarınız vardı. Bu gazeteyi belki hatırlıyorsunuz siz de, 2023 yılında aya gideceğimiz belirtiliyordu, şimdi bu 2028'e ertelendi, sizin açıklamanızda o şekilde bir ifade yer aldı. Gidildi mi, yoksa ne zaman olacak, aya gidiş ne zaman gerçekleşecek?
Yapılan yatırımları biz önemsiyoruz çünkü sanayici varsa işçi vardır, işçi varsa üretim vardır ama tüm metine baktığımız zaman, siz yaptığınız her çalışmayı anlatırken paylaştığınız düşünceler için de tüm çalışan işçilerin, emekçilerin emekleri için de bir teşekkürün yer almasını açıkçası ben bekliyordum ama ona rastlamadık. Belki konuşmanızın sonunda belirtirsiniz. İşçinin, üretimin olmadığı yerde sonuç elde edilemez.
Bir de endüstri 3.5'deyiz, endüstri 4.0'e daha geçemedik ama robotlarla ilgili çalışmalar var. Oradan doğan işsizliğin giderilmesi yönünde nasıl plan, proje çalışmaları var, onu da öğrenmek isterim.
Teşekkür ederim.
records.englishTranslation:
ÖMER FETHI GÜRER (Niğde) - Dear Minister, different segments of society are experiencing great difficulties due to inflation and tight monetary policy. Starting from 2024, serious problems will arise for industrialists due to the impact of high interest rates. The decline in the manufacturing industry continues, some textile factories are moving out of the country and some factories are disbanding. If this process turns into a permanent transformation process rather than a temporary contraction, the significant unemployment problem in Türkiye will deepen further. Factories state that the VAT they pay when purchasing raw materials causes an additional burden for industrialists from the production process to the marketing point, and that VAT rate differences negatively affect the buying and selling process. They request that you take action on this issue before the Ministry of Treasury and Finance. Administrative fines are also imposed by the Ministry of Industry and Technology for some practices. What is the total of these fines in the last two years and how much has been collected?
Problems with measuring and weighing instruments and packaging are also among consumer complaints. Inspection of measuring and weighing instruments should be carried out continuously and regularly. What has been done is not enough and complaints come to us. In this context, weight games in the packaging also mislead the consumer. Indirect price increases are achieved by keeping the container size the same and reducing the product weight. The producer thinks that he is buying the same product by looking at the size, but the citizens are also put into trouble in this aspect by the weight game.
Your activity report also states that there is a need to increase the number of competent personnel in the field of strengthening corporate culture and belonging, and that in-service training is needed to improve the competence of existing personnel. I think it is important for the staff to be competent and effective, and to meet the demands in this regard as you determine them yourself.
Also, Mr. Minister, there are projects that "have a name but no implementation" for my region. I would like to ask you once again what these projects are: At what stage is the Ulukışla District Industrial Park Project? What is the latest situation regarding the energy specialized industrial zone, which was announced to be established in Bor district of Niğde province in 2015? Niğde Bor Leather Integrated Industry
Although the Investment Project is an industrial zone, at what stage is it? Has any resources been allocated from the 2026 budget for the Niğde Bor Besi Organized Industrial Zone Project, in which Bor Chamber of Commerce and Industry and Bor Chamber of Agriculture are project partners, and is it included in the investment programs in this context? How many factories have ceased operations in the last two years? How many factories declared concordat? Is there any support in this context? The industrialist rightly explains the following problem regarding this concordat: The factory declares the concordat, but the others from whom it buys and makes purchases also fall into trouble along with it when the payment process is not completed. It is stated that there are problems in the management of this process. In accordance with the Industrial Registry Law No. 6948, how many of the companies and factories of industrial enterprises have been sold to foreign nationals, companies and organizations?

Although it is a Turkish brand, it is stated that many factories will be taken over by foreigners and companies will change hands in 2025. Do you have data on this? We would be pleased if you share this data with us.
Also, Mr. Minister, you had forward-looking definitions in most parts of your speech. Maybe you remember this newspaper, it was stated that we would go to the moon in 2023, now it has been postponed to 2028, and such a statement was included in your statement. Has it been done? If not, when will it happen? When will going to the moon happen?
We care about the investments made because if there are industrialists, there are workers, if there are workers, there is production, but when we look at the entire text, I was honestly expecting there to be a thank you for the efforts of all working workers and laborers, for the thoughts you shared while explaining every work you did, but we did not come across it. Maybe you can specify it at the end of your speech. Where there are no workers and no production, results cannot be achieved.
We are also in industry 3.5, we have not yet moved to industry 4.0, but there are studies on robots. I would also like to know what kind of plans and projects are there to eliminate the unemployment arising from there.
Thank you.

Bu verileri de bizimle paylaşırsanız memnun oluruz.
Ayrıca, Sayın Bakan, konuşmanızın çoğu yerinde geleceğe dönük tanımlamalarınız vardı. Bu gazeteyi belki hatırlıyorsunuz siz de, 2023 yılında aya gideceğimiz belirtiliyordu, şimdi bu 2028'e ertelendi, sizin açıklamanızda o şekilde bir ifade yer aldı. Gidildi mi, yoksa ne zaman olacak, aya gidiş ne zaman gerçekleşecek?
Yapılan yatırımları biz önemsiyoruz çünkü sanayici varsa işçi vardır, işçi varsa üretim vardır ama tüm metine baktığımız zaman, siz yaptığınız her çalışmayı anlatırken paylaştığınız düşünceler için de tüm çalışan işçilerin, emekçilerin emekleri için de bir teşekkürün yer almasını açıkçası ben bekliyordum ama ona rastlamadık. Belki konuşmanızın sonunda belirtirsiniz. İşçinin, üretimin olmadığı yerde sonuç elde edilemez.
Bir de endüstri 3.5'deyiz, endüstri 4.0'e daha geçemedik ama robotlarla ilgili çalışmalar var. Oradan doğan işsizliğin giderilmesi yönünde nasıl plan, proje çalışmaları var, onu da öğrenmek isterim.
Teşekkür ederim.
records.englishTranslation:
ÖMER FETHI GÜRER (Niğde) - Dear Minister, different segments of society are experiencing great difficulties due to inflation and tight monetary policy. Starting from 2024, serious problems will arise for industrialists due to the effect of high interest rates. The decline in the manufacturing industry continues, some textile factories are moving out of the country and some factories are disbanding. If this process turns into a permanent transformation process rather than a temporary contraction, the significant unemployment problem in Türkiye will deepen further. Factories state that the VAT they pay when purchasing raw materials causes an additional burden for industrialists from the production process to the marketing point, and that VAT rate differences negatively affect the buying and selling process. They request that you take action on this issue before the Ministry of Treasury and Finance. Administrative fines are also imposed by the Ministry of Industry and Technology for some practices. What is the total of these fines in the last two years and how much has been collected?
Problems with measuring and weighing instruments and packaging are also among consumer complaints. Inspection of measuring and weighing instruments should be carried out continuously and regularly. What has been done is not enough and complaints come to us. In this context, weight games in the packaging also mislead the consumer. Indirect price increases are achieved by keeping the container size the same and reducing the product weight. The producer thinks that he is buying the same product by looking at the size, but the citizens are also put into trouble in this aspect by the weight game.
Your activity report also states that there is a need to increase the number of competent personnel in the field of strengthening corporate culture and belonging, and that in-service training is needed to improve the competence of existing personnel. I think it is important for the staff to be competent and effective, and to meet the demands in this regard as you determine them yourself.
Also, Mr. Minister, there are projects that "have a name but no implementation" for my region. I would like to ask you once again what these projects are: At what stage is the Ulukışla District Industrial Park Project? What is the latest situation regarding the energy specialized industrial zone, which was announced to be established in Bor district of Niğde province in 2015? Niğde Bor Leather Integrated Industry
Although the Investment Project is an industrial zone, at what stage is it? Has any resources been allocated from the 2026 budget for the Niğde Bor Besi Organized Industrial Zone Project, in which Bor Chamber of Commerce and Industry and Bor Chamber of Agriculture are project partners, and is it included in the investment programs in this context? How many factories have ceased operations in the last two years? How many factories declared concordat? Is there any support in this context? The industrialist rightly explains the following problem regarding this concordat: The factory declares the concordat, but the others from whom it buys and makes purchases also fall into trouble along with it when the payment process is not completed. It is stated that there are problems in the management of this process. In accordance with the Industrial Registry Law No. 6948, how many of the companies and factories of industrial enterprises have been sold to foreign nationals, companies and organizations?

Although it is a Turkish brand, it is stated that many factories will be taken over by foreigners and companies will change hands in 2025. Do you have data on this? We would be pleased if you share this data with us.
Also, Mr. Minister, you had forward-looking definitions in most parts of your speech. Maybe you remember this newspaper, it was stated that we would go to the moon in 2023, now it has been postponed to 2028, and such a statement was included in your statement. Has it been done? If not, when will it happen? When will going to the moon happen?
We care about the investments made because if there are industrialists, there are workers, if there are workers, there is production, but when we look at the entire text, I was honestly expecting there to be a thank you for the efforts of all working workers and laborers, for the thoughts you shared while explaining every work you did, but we did not come across it. Maybe you can specify it at the end of your speech. Where there are no workers and no production, results cannot be achieved.
We are also in industry 3.5, we have not yet moved to industry 4.0, but there are studies on robots. I would also like to know what kind of plans and projects are there to eliminate the unemployment arising from there.
Thank you.

fields.Subject: 2026 Yılı Merkezi Yönetim Bütçe Kanunu Teklifi (1/280) ve 2024 Yılı Merkezi Yönetim Kesin Hesap Kanunu Teklifi (1/279) ile Sayıştay tezkereleri münasebetiyle

records.originalTurkish:
ÖMER FETHİ GÜRER (Niğde) - Sayın Bakan,
esnaf rafta sattığı ürünün yenisini alıp koymaya zorlanıyor, borç döndürmede sıkıntı var. AVM'lerin baskısıyla esnafların çoğu işi bıraktı. Perakende yasasının kapsamlı olarak düzenlenmemesi esnafı iyice perişan etti. Esnaf, çiftçi 9000 prim gününü 7200 güne düşürme sözü yerine getirilmediği için bizlere de sizlere iletmek için tepkilerini iletiyor. Esnaf prim ödemede zorlanıyor, krediyi geri döndürmede zorlanıyor, esnafların sorunları arttı. Bu arada, marketlere doğrudan gidip sözleşmeli üretim yapma yetkisi verildi. Gidiyorlar çiftçilerimize üretim yaptırıyorlar, sonra halden çıkan üründen daha pahalı fiyatla bu ürünleri marketlerde satıyorlar. Aracılık sistemini kaldırdık derken marketler aracının payının da fazlasını almaya başladı. Bu konularda bir düzenlemeye ihtiyaç var, bunu değerlendirmenize sunuyorum.
Sayın Bakan, lokanta ve kafe esnafı "Biz yüzde 1 KDV'yle aldığımız ürünlerde sonra yüzde 10 KDV ödeyerek değerlendirme kapsamı içindeyiz." diyor, bunun da düzeltilmesini bekliyorlar. Ayrıca, kiraların çok yüksek olduğunu belirtiyorlar. Bu arada, Tarım Kredi Kooperatifleri birdenbire logolarını değiştirdi, tabelasını değiştirdi, reyonunu değiştirdi. Niye ihtiyaç duydu da boş yere binlerce lira parayı harcadı? Güya 3 harfli marketlere rakip olacaktı, şimdi onlardan daha pahalı satıyor. Bu konuda ne kadar para harcandı? Niye ihtiyaç duyuldu? Bu konuda da bir açıklamanız olursa memnun olurum.
Dahilde işleme rejimi kapsamında ülkemize ürün geliyor. Bu gelen ürünlerden 2024-2025 yıllarında hangi gıda ürünleri geldi? Bunlar işlendikten sonra yurt dışına ne olarak gönderildi? Örneğin, buğday un ve makarna oluyor, sonra kalkıp "Bakın, biz yurt dışından ürün getirdik, bunu işledik, yurt dışına sattık, dünya 1'incisiyiz, Avrupa 1'incisiyiz." filan deniliyor. İyi de diğer ülkeler onları satarken bizim fiyatımızın 3 katına satıyor, biz en ucuz unu, makarnayı verdiğimizden 1'inciliği biz alıyoruz. Niye katma değerli ürüne dönüştürdüğümüzde daha uygun fiyatta ve daha çok gelir sağlayacak marka ürünleri yaratma yolunda çalışmalarımız olmuyor? Piyasayı dengelemek için ithalat yapılıyor. İthalat piyasayı dengelemiyor, piyasadaki üründen fazla fiyatla satılıyor. Örneğin, ülkemizde Osmaniye'de yetişen yer fıstığının 2 katı fiyatına markette, rafta ithal Kanada fıstığı satılıyor. Yurt dışından gelen ürün Türkiye'dekinden daha pahalıya satılıp tüketici mağdur edilecekse bu ithalatın önüne bir geçelim yani onun yanında yerli üreticiyi destekleyelim, ülkemiz üreticisi üretsin. Bu ithalatın bu kadar kapısı açık bırakılıyor. Peki, ihracatta... Şimdi patates tarlada kaldı. Niye bunun yurt dışına ihracatı için bir çalışma yapılmaz da bu konuda düzenlemeye gidilmez? Ayrıca, zeytinyağı gibi, salça gibi ürünlerde ihracat izni niye döneminde değil de daha sonraki süreçte üretimin önünde engel oluşturacak dönemde veriliyor? Bu sefer de yurt dışı piyasası yok oluyor.
TİCARET BAKANI ÖMER BOLAT - Sayın Vekilim, iki ay önce ihracat teşviki verdik patatese, soğana.
ÖMER FETHİ GÜRER (Niğde) - Bakın, iki dakikam gitti, onu sonunda açıklayayım.
TİCARET BAKANI ÖMER BOLAT - Senin bilmen lazım, Niğdelisin.
ÖMER FETHİ GÜRER (Niğde) - Biliyorum ama onunla ilgili kurallar esnetilmediği için sorun var.
Sayın Bakanım, şu TURQUALITY'e gelelim.
CAVİT ARI (Antalya) - Sayın Bakanım, bazı yerlerde patates ekimi yasaklandı, haberiniz yok.
ÖMER FETHİ GÜRER (Niğde) - Bir dakika Cavit Bey, vakit geçiyor ya.
Sayın Bakan, TURQUALITY'le ilgili daha önce de sordum, Türk firmalarına Türk markası olsun diye verdiğiniz desteği, bu firmalar satıldı, satıldıktan sonra yabancı firmaya devam ettirdiniz. Bu para ne kadar? Yani İngiliz'in, Fransız'ın firmasına Türk markasına verilen destek niye verildi? Bunları niye geri talep etmediniz? Satanlardan niye tahsil etmediniz? Amacınız neydi? Dünyada Türk markası yaratmak. Türk markası yaratacağım dediğiniz firmalar gidip satıldı, onlara ödediğimiz para da ülkemizin zarar hanesine yazıldı. Bunların tahsilatı ve ne kadar olduğu konusunu soruyorum, yanıt alamıyorum. Ayrıca, Türk markası yaratılma olgusundan vazgeçtik, Türk firmaları satılıyor. Son dönemde marka olmuş 30'a yakın firmamız yabancı şirketlere satıldı. Bunları önleyecek, bunlara destek olacak çalışmalara ihtiyaç olduğunu görüyorum.
Yöresel ürünlerin perakende sektöründe yer bulması için reyonlarda açılması gereken alanlar olduğunu belirtiyorsunuz ancak bu yerel ürünler küçük aile tipi işletmelerden alınmıyor, büyük şirketler eliyle ya da o bölgedeki aracılardan alınıyor. Doğrudan zincir marketlerde yerel üreticinin ürününü getirip satması olası değil çünkü marketler reyon parası istiyor, raf başı istiyor, onu istiyor, bunu istiyor, gariban yine oradan fayda sağlamıyor. Eğer küçük üretici desteklenecekse Ticaret Bakanlığının belirlediği kriterler olmalı, onun üzerinden oralarda ürünlerin
satışı sağlanmalı. Esnafı sorunlu, çiftçisi sorunlu, üreticisi sorunlu, besicisi sorunlu ve bunun yanında asgari ücretlisi, dar gelirlisi, ekonomik anlamda problem yaşayanların sorunlu olduğu süreçte en azından satılan üründe fahiş fiyatla ilgili denetim yaptığınızı söylüyorsunuz.
(Mikrofon otomatik cihaz tarafından kapatıldı)
ÖMER FETHİ GÜRER (Niğde) - Fahiş fiyatlı neyi denetleseniz fiyatı daha da fahiş oluyor çünkü alıyorlar, o fiyatları da ayrıca getirip ürünün üzerine ekliyorlar ve üründen çıkarıyorlar cezayı. Kestiğiniz cezaların ne kadarını tahsil ettiniz? Rakamlar ve tahsilat nedir, onu da öğrenmek istiyorum.
Teşekkür ederim.
records.englishTranslation:
ÖMER FETHI GÜRER (Niğde) - Dear Minister,
Tradesmen are forced to buy new ones for the products they sell on the shelves, and there is a problem in repaying debts. Most of the tradesmen quit their jobs under the pressure of shopping malls. The fact that the retail law was not regulated comprehensively made the tradesmen very miserable. Tradesmen and farmers are sending their reactions to us and to you as the promise to reduce the 9000 premium days to 7200 days has not been fulfilled. Tradesmen are having difficulty paying premiums, having difficulty returning loans, and the problems of tradesmen have increased. Meanwhile, markets were given the authority to go directly to markets and carry out contract production. They go to our farmers and make them produce, then they sell these products in markets at a higher price than the finished product. As we abolished the intermediary system, markets started to take more than the share of the intermediary. There is a need for regulation on these issues, I submit this for your consideration.
Dear Minister, restaurant and cafe shopkeepers said, "We are within the scope of evaluation by paying 10 percent VAT on the products we buy with 1 percent VAT." He says, they expect this to be fixed as well. They also state that rents are too high. Meanwhile, Agricultural Credit Cooperatives suddenly changed their logos, changed their signs, and changed their sections. Why did he need it and spent thousands of liras for nothing? Supposedly it was going to compete with 3-letter markets, but now it sells more expensively than them. How much money was spent on this? Why was it needed? I would be grateful if you could have an explanation on this matter.
Products come to our country within the scope of inward processing regime. Which food products arrived in 2024-2025? After they were processed, how were they sent abroad? For example, wheat becomes flour and pasta, then you stand up and say, "Look, we brought products from abroad, we processed it, we sold it abroad, we are the 1st in the world, the 1st in Europe." It is said so and so. Well, other countries sell them at 3 times our price, but since we offer the cheapest flour and pasta, we take the first place. Why don't we work on creating branded products that are more affordable and will generate more income when we turn them into value-added products? Imports are made to balance the market. Imports do not balance the market, they are sold at a higher price than the product in the market. For example, imported Canadian peanuts are sold in the market and on the shelves at twice the price of peanuts grown in Osmaniye in our country. If the product coming from abroad is sold at a higher price than the product from Turkey and the consumer will be victimized, let's prevent this import, that is, let's support the domestic producer and let our country's producer produce. The door to this import is left open. Well, in exports. Now the potatoes are left in the field. Why is there no work done to export this product abroad and no regulations are made on this issue? Moreover, why are export permits for products such as olive oil and tomato paste given not in the current period, but in a period that will create an obstacle to production in the future? This time, the foreign market is disappearing.
MINISTER OF TRADE ÖMER BOLAT - Dear Deputy, two months ago we gave export incentives to potatoes and onions.
ÖMER FETHI GÜRER (Niğde) - Look, I just spent two minutes, let me explain it at last.
MINISTER OF TRADE ÖMER BOLAT - You should know, you are from Niğde.

ÖMER FETHI GÜRER (Niğde) - I know, but there is a problem because the rules regarding it are not relaxed.
Dear Minister, let's talk about TURQUALITY.
CAVİT ARI (Antalya) - Dear Minister, potato planting is banned in some places, you are not aware of it.
ÖMER FETHI GÜRER (Niğde) - Wait a minute, Mr. Cavit, time is passing.
Mr. Minister, I asked about TURQUALITY before, the support you gave to Turkish companies to become a Turkish brand, these companies were sold, and after they were sold, you continued the support to foreign companies. How much is this money? In other words, why was the support given to a British or French company given to a Turkish brand? Why didn't you demand these back? Why didn't you collect from the sellers? What was your aim? Creating a Turkish brand in the world. The companies that you said would create a Turkish brand were sold, and the money we paid for them was written off as a loss for our country. I ask about the collection of these and how much they are, but I cannot get an answer. In addition, we have given up on creating a Turkish brand, Turkish companies are being sold. Recently, nearly 30 of our branded companies have been sold to foreign companies. I see that there is a need for studies that will prevent and support these.
You state that there are areas that need to be opened in the aisles for local products to find a place in the retail industry, but these local products are not purchased from small family-type businesses, they are purchased by large companies or from intermediaries in that region. It is not possible for local producers to bring and sell their products directly in chain markets, because markets want section money, they want shelf space, they want this, they want that, but the poor people still do not benefit from it. If small producers are to be supported, there must be criteria determined by the Ministry of Commerce, and through that the products will be sold there.
sale should be ensured. You say that you at least inspected the exorbitant prices of the products sold during the period when the tradesmen were problematic, the farmers were problematic, the producers were problematic, the breeders were problematic, as well as the minimum wage earners, low-income earners, and those with economic problems were problematic.
(The microphone was automatically turned off by the device)
ÖMER FETHİ GÜRER (Niğde) - Whatever you inspect at exorbitant prices, the price becomes even more exorbitant because they buy it, add those prices to the product and subtract the penalty from the product. How much of the fines you imposed have you collected? I also want to learn what the numbers and collection are.
Thank you.

Yöresel ürünlerin perakende sektöründe yer bulması için reyonlarda açılması gereken alanlar olduğunu belirtiyorsunuz ancak bu yerel ürünler küçük aile tipi işletmelerden alınmıyor, büyük şirketler eliyle ya da o bölgedeki aracılardan alınıyor. Doğrudan zincir marketlerde yerel üreticinin ürününü getirip satması olası değil çünkü marketler reyon parası istiyor, raf başı istiyor, onu istiyor, bunu istiyor, gariban yine oradan fayda sağlamıyor. Eğer küçük üretici desteklenecekse Ticaret Bakanlığının belirlediği kriterler olmalı, onun üzerinden oralarda ürünlerin
satışı sağlanmalı. Esnafı sorunlu, çiftçisi sorunlu, üreticisi sorunlu, besicisi sorunlu ve bunun yanında asgari ücretlisi, dar gelirlisi, ekonomik anlamda problem yaşayanların sorunlu olduğu süreçte en azından satılan üründe fahiş fiyatla ilgili denetim yaptığınızı söylüyorsunuz.
(Mikrofon otomatik cihaz tarafından kapatıldı)
ÖMER FETHİ GÜRER (Niğde) - Fahiş fiyatlı neyi denetleseniz fiyatı daha da fahiş oluyor çünkü alıyorlar, o fiyatları da ayrıca getirip ürünün üzerine ekliyorlar ve üründen çıkarıyorlar cezayı. Kestiğiniz cezaların ne kadarını tahsil ettiniz? Rakamlar ve tahsilat nedir, onu da öğrenmek istiyorum.
Teşekkür ederim.
records.englishTranslation:
ÖMER FETHI GÜRER (Niğde) - Dear Minister,
Tradesmen are forced to buy new ones for the products they sell on the shelves, and there is a problem in repaying debts. Most of the tradesmen quit their jobs under the pressure of shopping malls. The fact that the retail law was not regulated comprehensively made the tradesmen very miserable. Tradesmen and farmers are sending their reactions to us and to you as the promise to reduce the 9000 premium days to 7200 days has not been fulfilled. Tradesmen are having difficulty paying premiums, having difficulty returning loans, and the problems of tradesmen have increased. Meanwhile, markets were given the authority to go directly to markets and carry out contract production. They go to our farmers and make them produce, then they sell these products in markets at a higher price than the finished product. As we abolished the intermediary system, markets started to take more than the share of the intermediary. There is a need for regulation on these issues, I submit this for your consideration.
Dear Minister, restaurant and cafe shopkeepers said, "We are within the scope of evaluation by paying 10 percent VAT on the products we buy with 1 percent VAT." He says, they expect this to be fixed as well. They also state that rents are too high. Meanwhile, Agricultural Credit Cooperatives suddenly changed their logos, changed their signs, and changed their sections. Why did he need it and spent thousands of liras for nothing? Supposedly it was going to compete with 3-letter markets, but now it sells more expensively than them. How much money was spent on this? Why was it needed? I would be grateful if you could have an explanation on this matter.
Products come to our country within the scope of inward processing regime. Which food products arrived in 2024-2025? After they were processed, how were they sent abroad? For example, wheat becomes flour and pasta, then you stand up and say, "Look, we brought products from abroad, we processed it, we sold it abroad, we are the 1st in the world, the 1st in Europe." It is said so and so. Well, other countries sell them at 3 times our price, but since we offer the cheapest flour and pasta, we take the first place. Why don't we work on creating branded products that are more affordable and will generate more income when we turn them into value-added products? Imports are made to balance the market. Imports do not balance the market, they are sold at a higher price than the product in the market. For example, imported Canadian peanuts are sold in the market and on the shelves at twice the price of peanuts grown in Osmaniye in our country. If the product coming from abroad is sold at a higher price than the product from Turkey and the consumer will be victimized, let's prevent this import, that is, let's support the domestic producer and let our country's producer produce. The door to this import is left open. Well, in exports. Now the potatoes are left in the field. Why is there no work done to export this product abroad and no regulations are made on this issue? Moreover, why are export permits for products such as olive oil and tomato paste given not in the current period, but in a period that will create an obstacle to production in the future? This time, the foreign market is disappearing.
MINISTER OF TRADE ÖMER BOLAT - Dear Deputy, two months ago we gave export incentives to potatoes and onions.
ÖMER FETHI GÜRER (Niğde) - Look, I just spent two minutes, let me explain it at last.
MINISTER OF TRADE ÖMER BOLAT - You should know, you are from Niğde.

ÖMER FETHI GÜRER (Niğde) - I know, but there is a problem because the rules regarding it are not relaxed.
Dear Minister, let's talk about TURQUALITY.
CAVİT ARI (Antalya) - Dear Minister, potato planting is banned in some places, you are not aware of it.
ÖMER FETHI GÜRER (Niğde) - Wait a minute, Mr. Cavit, time is passing.
Mr. Minister, I asked about TURQUALITY before, the support you gave to Turkish companies to become a Turkish brand, these companies were sold, and after they were sold, you continued the support to foreign companies. How much is this money? In other words, why was the support given to a British or French company given to a Turkish brand? Why didn't you demand these back? Why didn't you collect from the sellers? What was your aim? Creating a Turkish brand in the world. The companies that you said would create a Turkish brand were sold, and the money we paid for them was written off as a loss for our country. I ask about the collection of these and how much they are, but I cannot get an answer. In addition, we have given up on creating a Turkish brand, Turkish companies are being sold. Recently, nearly 30 of our branded companies have been sold to foreign companies. I see that there is a need for studies that will prevent and support these.
You state that there are areas that need to be opened in the aisles for local products to find a place in the retail industry, but these local products are not purchased from small family-type businesses, they are purchased by large companies or from intermediaries in that region. It is not possible for local producers to bring and sell their products directly in chain markets, because markets want section money, they want shelf space, they want this, they want that, but the poor people still do not benefit from it. If small producers are to be supported, there must be criteria determined by the Ministry of Commerce, and through that the products will be sold there.
sale should be ensured. You say that you at least inspected the exorbitant prices of the products sold during the period when the tradesmen were problematic, the farmers were problematic, the producers were problematic, the breeders were problematic, as well as the minimum wage earners, low-income earners, and those with economic problems were problematic.
(The microphone was automatically turned off by the device)
ÖMER FETHİ GÜRER (Niğde) - Whatever you inspect at exorbitant prices, the price becomes even more exorbitant because they buy it, add those prices to the product and subtract the penalty from the product. How much of the fines you imposed have you collected? I also want to learn what the numbers and collection are.
Thank you.

records.originalTurkish:
ÖMER FETHİ GÜRER (Niğde) - Teşekkürler Başkanım.
Sayın Bakanım, size 2 tane mektup okuyarak konuya girmek istiyorum: "Aldığım maaş 18 bin lira emekli maaşı ve bir artı bir daire için ödediğim kira aylık 10 bin lira. Ev kirasıyla ciddi anlamda zorlanıyorum, markete haftada bir kez korkarak gidiyorum, dört yıldır sabah kahvaltısı yapmıyorum. Kızım bu yıl fen lisesini bitirdi ve üniversite sınavında iyi bir derece yaparak İngilizce eğitim veren Ankara Üniversitesi Siyasal Bilgiler Fakültesini kazandı. Lütfen, bin lira da olsa az çok demeden maddi yardımınızı bekliyorum." Mustafa Bey böyle yazmış.
Diğer bir mesajda da diyor ki: "Sayın vekilim, bu mesajı yazarken çok düşündüm ama başka çarem de kalmadı. Artık bittim, tükendim. Ailemin, eşimin, çocuğumun, herkesin hayatını mahvettim. Bankaya borçlarımdan dolayı artık nefes bile alamıyorum. Tek başıma çıkamıyorum, sizlerden yardım istiyorum." Suat Bey de böyle yazmış. 5 bine yakın ortalama mail alıyoruz. Size bir önerim var: Ülkenin içinde bulunduğu durumun ne olduğunu daha iyi anlamanız için yalnız sizin adınıza bir mail açın, çarşıya pazara çıkmayın, durumu görürsünüz.
Emeklinin maaşı yetmiyor, asgari ücretli açlık sınırının altında, geçinemiyor.
Taşerona 2026 yılında yine kadro görünmüyor.
Staj ve çırak TÜİK'te işçi sayılıyor, sigorta kartı veriliyor ancak yaşlılık sigortası başlatılmıyor, en az dört yılı çalınıyor, onlarla ilgili de burada bir düzenleme 2026 yılı için görünmüyor.
EYT çıktı ama kademeli bir geçiş olmadı, bir günle on yedi yıl kaybedenler var. Bu bütçede bu kesimlerle ilgili de bir düzenleme yok. Siz bu kesimlere nasıl bakıyorsunuz, öğrenmek istiyorum.
Çiftçilerimizin ise yaşadıkları mağduriyet her geçen gün katlıyor. Çiftçilere millî gelirin yüzde 1'i 2026 yılında 722 milyar yaparken ayırdığınız destek 168 milyar lira ama başka bir kötülük yapıyorsunuz, düşük alım fiyatıyla çiftçiyi tüccarın eline mahkûm ediyorsunuz ve bu yüzden üretim düşüyor. 2023 yılından bugüne hububatta 10 milyon ton geriye düşmemiz var, buğdayda 4 milyon tonu bulmuş. Böyle giderse arz açığı katlayacak, ithalatçı olmaya devam edeceğiz. Meyvede bir yıllık kaybımız 8 milyon ton, kayıt dışıyla 10 milyon ton. Mayıs ayında Merkez Bankası Başkanı buraya geldi "Zirai donun etkisi olmayacak." dedi, Tarım ve Orman Bakanı "Arz açığımız oluşmayacak." dedi, sonra da ortalık ekonomik anlamda bozulunca gerçek olan, adalet ve hukuktan kopmuş yönetim tarzının yarattığı sorun zirai don ile kuraklığa endekslendi ama zirai donda da TARSİM'e kayıtlı olanlara dahi alması gereken destek verilmedi.
YAŞAR KIRKPINAR (İzmir) - Verildi, verildi.
ÖMER FETHİ GÜRER (Niğde) - ÇKS'si olmayan çiftçilere şu anda zirai don desteği de verilmiyor; bahçelerine bakamıyorlar, gübre alamıyorlar, ilaç atamıyorlar, budama yapamıyorlar, gelecek yılları da kaybediyoruz. Şu anda bankalara çiftçilerin borcu 1 trilyon 117 milyar lira. 10 binden fazla araziye icra gelmiş, bine yakın traktör icra yoluyla çiftçinin elinden alınıyor. Sayın Bakan, gideceğiniz bir şehir olursa yediemin depolarına bir uğrayın, traktörlerin orada bağlı olduğunu görürsünüz. 2024 yılında 3 milyar 600 milyon lira takipteki borç şu anda 10 milyar lirayı bulmuş.
Çiftçinin, işçinin, esnafın, emeklinin, dar gelirlinin içinde olmadığı, verginin, faizin ve geniş kesimlerin yoksulluğunu artıran bir bütçe teklifiyle geldiniz. Bu ülkenin insanlarına bu kadar niye zulmediyorsunuz? Yokluğu ve yoksulluğu derinleştiriyorsunuz; çocuklar okullara aç gidiyor, orada yemek yemeden dönüyor.
Farklı biçimde yarattığınız olumsuzlukların ülkenin genel dokusunu bozduğundan haberiniz var mı? Boşanmalar arttı, madde bağımlılığı arttı, yuvalar dağılıyor, aile yuvası kurmak isteyen gençler ondan vazgeçmişler. Ülkenin içine düşürüldüğü durumda önemli ölçüde sorunlar katlanmış. Bu ülkeyi nereye sürüklüyorsunuz? Bu anlamda yapılanları biz yerinde gidiyor, görüyoruz. Bakın, size bir önerim var: Şu Devlet Demiryollarının yüksek hızlı trende kadrosuz, düşük maaşla çalıştırdığınız elemanların derdi var ama başka bir iş yapın, gelin, Antalya'dan Ankara'ya, İstanbul'a demir yolu projesi geliştirin de en azından sera ürünleri, gıda ürünleri nakliyeyle gelen artıştan
muaf olsun, bu bölgelere demir yoluyla gıda taşıması yapalım. Gıdada arz açığımızın artmasının yanında sorunlarımızın giderek derinleştiğini belirteyim. Bir gün cepte para yoksa rafta ürün olması bu ülkenin hayrına değil diyor, teşekkür ediyorum.
records.englishTranslation:
ÖMER FETHI GÜRER (Niğde) - Thank you, President.
Dear Minister, I would like to get into the subject by reading you two letters: "The salary I receive is 18 thousand liras as a pension and the rent I pay for an apartment plus one is 10 thousand liras per month. I have serious difficulties with the house rent, I go to the market once a week with fear, I have not had breakfast for four years. My daughter graduated from the science high school this year and got a good grade in the university exam and got into Ankara University Faculty of Political Sciences, which teaches in English. Please, even if it is a thousand liras, it is more or less financial. I am waiting for your help." Mustafa Bey wrote like this.
In another message, he says: "Dear deputy, I thought a lot while writing this message, but I had no other choice. I am finished, exhausted. I have ruined the lives of my family, my wife, my child, everyone. I can't even breathe anymore because of my debts to the bank. I cannot go out alone, I ask for your help." Suat Bey also wrote like this. We receive an average of nearly 5 thousand e-mails. I have a suggestion for you: To better understand the situation of the country, open an e-mail in your name alone, do not go to the market, you will see the situation.
The pensioner's salary is not enough, the minimum wage is below the hunger line, he cannot make ends meet.
There will be no staff for the subcontractor again in 2026.
Interns and apprentices are considered workers in TURKSTAT, an insurance card is issued, but old-age insurance is not initiated, at least four years are stolen, and there is no regulation regarding them here for 2026.
EYT was released but there was no gradual transition, there are people who lost seventeen years in one day. There is no regulation regarding these segments in this budget. I want to know how you view these segments.
The grievances experienced by our farmers are increasing day by day. The support you allocate to farmers is 168 billion liras, while 1 percent of the national income will amount to 722 billion in 2026, but you are doing another evil, you are condemning the farmers to the hands of the merchants with the low purchase price, and therefore production decreases. We have fallen behind by 10 million tons in grain since 2023, and it has reached 4 million tons in wheat. If it continues like this, the supply deficit will multiply and we will continue to be importers. Our annual loss in fruit is 8 million tons, 10 million tons unregistered. The President of the Central Bank came here in May and said, "There will be no effect of agricultural frost." said the Minister of Agriculture and Forestry. "We will not have a supply deficit." He said, and then when things got worse economically, the problem created by the management style that was disconnected from justice and law was indexed to agricultural frost and drought, but even those registered with TARSİM were not given the support they should have received during the agricultural frost.
YAŞAR KIRKPINAR (İzmir) - It was given, it was given.
ÖMER FETHI GÜRER (Niğde) - Currently, agricultural donor support is not given to farmers who do not have a ÇKS; They cannot take care of their gardens, they cannot buy fertilizer, they cannot apply pesticides, they cannot prune, and we are losing the coming years. Currently, farmers' debt to banks is 1 trillion 117 billion liras. More than 10 thousand lands have been seized, and nearly a thousand tractors are being taken away from farmers through enforcement. Dear Minister, if you go to a city, stop by the trustee warehouses and you will see the tractors tied there. The outstanding debt of 3 billion 600 million liras in 2024 has now reached 10 billion liras.

You came with a budget proposal that does not include farmers, workers, tradesmen, retirees and low-income people, and increases the poverty of large segments of people due to taxes, interest. Why do you oppress the people of this country so much? You are deepening poverty and want; Children go to school hungry and return without eating.
Are you aware that the negativities you create in different ways are disrupting the general fabric of the country? Divorces have increased, substance abuse has increased, homes are falling apart, young people who wanted to start a family have given up on it. The problems that the country has been reduced to have increased significantly. Where are you dragging this country? In this sense, we are seeing what is being done on the spot. Look, I have a suggestion for you: This State Railways has a problem with the unemployed, low-paid employees you employ on the high-speed train, but do something else, come and develop a railway project from Antalya to Ankara to Istanbul, so that at least greenhouse products and food products can be saved from the increase in transportation.
Let's be exempt, let's transport food to these regions by rail. Let me state that our problems are getting deeper as our food supply deficit increases. One day, if there is no money in your pocket, I say it is not good for this country to have products on the shelves, I thank you.

ÖMER FETHI GÜRER (Niğde) - Thank you, President.
Dear Minister, I would like to get into the subject by reading you two letters: "The salary I receive is 18 thousand liras as a pension and the rent I pay for an apartment plus one is 10 thousand liras per month. I have serious difficulties with the house rent, I go to the market once a week with fear, I have not had breakfast for four years. My daughter graduated from the science high school this year and got a good grade in the university exam and got into Ankara University Faculty of Political Sciences, which teaches in English. Please, even if it is a thousand liras, it is more or less financial. I am waiting for your help." Mustafa Bey wrote like this.
In another message, he says: "Dear deputy, I thought a lot while writing this message, but I had no other choice. I am finished, exhausted. I have ruined the lives of my family, my wife, my child, everyone. I can't even breathe anymore because of my debts to the bank. I cannot go out alone, I ask for your help." Suat Bey also wrote like this. We receive an average of nearly 5 thousand e-mails. I have a suggestion for you: To better understand the situation of the country, open an e-mail in your name alone, do not go to the market, you will see the situation.
The pensioner's salary is not enough, the minimum wage is below the hunger line, he cannot make ends meet.
There will be no staff for the subcontractor again in 2026.
Interns and apprentices are considered workers in TURKSTAT, an insurance card is issued, but old-age insurance is not initiated, at least four years are stolen, and there is no regulation regarding them here for 2026.
EYT was released but there was no gradual transition, there are people who lost seventeen years in one day. There is no regulation regarding these segments in this budget. I want to know how you view these segments.
The grievances experienced by our farmers are increasing day by day. The support you allocate to farmers is 168 billion liras, while 1 percent of the national income will amount to 722 billion in 2026, but you are doing another evil, you are condemning the farmers to the hands of the merchants with the low purchase price, and therefore production decreases. We have fallen behind by 10 million tons in grain since 2023, and it has reached 4 million tons in wheat. If it continues like this, the supply deficit will multiply and we will continue to be importers. Our annual loss in fruit is 8 million tons, 10 million tons unregistered. The President of the Central Bank came here in May and said, "There will be no effect of agricultural frost." said the Minister of Agriculture and Forestry. "We will not have a supply deficit." He said, and then when things got worse economically, the problem created by the management style that was disconnected from justice and law was indexed to agricultural frost and drought, but even those registered with TARSİM were not given the support they should have received during the agricultural frost.
YAŞAR KIRKPINAR (İzmir) - It was given, it was given.
ÖMER FETHI GÜRER (Niğde) - Currently, agricultural donor support is not given to farmers who do not have a ÇKS; They cannot take care of their gardens, they cannot buy fertilizer, they cannot apply pesticides, they cannot prune, and we are losing the coming years. Currently, farmers' debt to banks is 1 trillion 117 billion lira. More than 10 thousand lands have been seized, and nearly a thousand tractors are being taken away from farmers through enforcement. Dear Minister, if you go to a city, stop by the trustee warehouses and you will see the tractors tied there. The outstanding debt of 3 billion 600 million liras in 2024 has now reached 10 billion liras.

You came with a budget proposal that does not include farmers, workers, tradesmen, retirees and low-income people, and increases the poverty of large segments of people due to taxes, interest. Why do you oppress the people of this country so much? You are deepening poverty and want; Children go to school hungry and return without eating.
Are you aware that the negativities you create in different ways are disrupting the general fabric of the country? Divorces have increased, substance abuse has increased, homes are falling apart, young people who wanted to start a family have given up on it. The problems that the country has been reduced to have increased significantly. Where are you dragging this country? In this sense, we are seeing what is being done on the spot. Look, I have a suggestion for you: This State Railways has a problem with the unemployed, low-paid employees you employ on the high-speed train, but do something else, come and develop a railway project from Antalya to Ankara to Istanbul, so that at least greenhouse products and food products can be saved from the increase in transportation.
Let's be exempt, let's transport food to these regions by rail. Let me state that our problems are getting deeper as our food supply deficit increases. One day, if there is no money in your pocket, I say it is not good for this country to have products on the shelves, I thank you.
